A rabid coyote attacked multiple people and dogs near NYC, while a fire on Staten Island prompted a significant FDNY response; additionally, NYC's Mayor Mamdani established an LGBTQIA office and faced scrutiny over various community issues, including complaints about a new broker fee ban and safety concerns in schools.

### Risks & Alerts

- Rabid coyote attacks 3 people and 6 dogs north of NYC; residents advised to stay alert. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Fire at a 3-story building on Staten Island prompts a large FDNY response; avoid the area. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- NYC fields hundreds of complaints over new broker fee ban, with only 2 refunds issued so far. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiyAFBVV95cUxOZWFDanliZDVYc0dteW9Tc3M3SUhncjg3Nl93a2pxTDFOZkdkc0RqWEh4ZWtYMUx1bmY5TFl5QUFIdUZ3M2IxTTl5V2NFZEpidm1oN2kzM3JZZ25iTUxQcEZudDFId2NvVk1TQTMzRlhlbWZJMFI0UDB5QkxNcXFZdVpTYWQ1SE0yV3J4R09PWVhQZXVSbnl4QTZfLUV2S24yYnh3LURxdzVXMzVlSEtyTFh2OE9BZy12bEh3RU4zbXI5aEdjcFJxdQ?oc=5)
- Mayor Mamdani creates NYC's first LGBTQIA office, led by an out trans agency head. ### Opportunities & Welfare

- NYC is giving out 5,000 free movie tickets during the new Art House Cinema Week. ### Community

- NYC TV legend Ernie Anastos has passed away at the age of 82. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- A free Hannah Montana pop-up featuring the show's glam closet is open in NYC.
